round_note: Refire on 2026-04-27 against the live tree. Verdict remains negative ROI for any direct port motion, and the live anima tree now weakens the port case further because `clm_lore_serve.hexa` mirrors a minimal in-memory session slice instead of reusing `serving/session_manager.hexa` as a shared substrate.

alpha.consumed_how: byte_hash[0] mod 4 keeps the declared option order; no late reordering after evidence review

beta.consumed_how: elevated the cheaper-alternative test from soft preference to mandatory gate; a dominant no-port alternative had to be named explicitly

gamma.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_lhc invocation=tool/lhc_opendata_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)
gamma.consumed_how: accepted OFFLINE-FALLBACK because this is an in-repo ROI question; no external fetch was needed or claimed

core_5op_kernel: 3) fan out K>=3 orthogonal axes (raw 48 correlation < 0.7)
5) emit witness JSON to declared landing path

load_bearing_semantics: compaction summaries and branch summaries in LLM context reconstruction
fast header reads for metadata-only listing
session listing/open/continue/fork flows used across runtime and tests

surface_markers: 5 structs: SessionMessage, ChatSession, SessionStore, SessionLookup, AppendResult
load_bearing_semantics: single linear message list, no tree
persistence only for simple chat history reconstruction

live_tree_nuance.evidence: Section 5 is explicitly labeled 'mirror of serving/session_manager.hexa, minimal' and redefines ChatSession + session_create + session_append locally.
live_tree_nuance.impact: Even within anima, the minimal session substrate is not yet the single shared dependency. That reduces the credibility of spending more effort porting the richer TS session engine across runtimes.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: direction-disambiguated
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: ROI is invalid unless the cycle scores concrete source->target motions rather than vague bidirectional 'porting'.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: feature-parity-not-overstated
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: If the TS surface contains migration/tree/compaction/branch/custom-message semantics not required by the target, a direct port claim must fail.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: target-runtime-constraints-respected
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: If the target runtime has documented constraints that force redesign rather than transliteration, direct-port ROI must fail.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: caller-demand-evidenced
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: At least one live cross-runtime consumer or roadmap need must require the port; otherwise the motion is demand-free.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: cheaper-alternative-not-dominant
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: A port-positive strategy fails if a cheaper no-port option dominates it.

options.name: TS->hexa direct port
options.reason: would require re-expressing a JSONL tree/migration/compaction engine inside a runtime whose current session substrate is a 30-turn linear chat window

options.name: hexa->TS direct port
options.reason: the hexa behavior is already a strict subset of what the TS manager and surrounding runtime already exceed

options.name: shared intersection schema, no code port
options.reason: only becomes useful if a real cross-runtime session interchange consumer appears

options.name: thin bridge/RPC to rich TS side
options.reason: still lacks a witnessed consumer and is more complex than current needs

notable_cells.S1.F2: TS carries tree traversal, compaction, branch summaries, migration, labels, session metadata, and custom context entries; hexa does not need or consume this surface.
notable_cells.S1.F3: hexa file header explicitly documents pass-by-value lists, aliasing hazards, and JSON avoidance; these force redesign rather than faithful transliteration.
notable_cells.S1.F4: roadmap TSRV-P1-1 is already satisfied by the existing hexa module; no task asks for TS feature parity.
notable_cells.S2.F5: copying the hexa subset into TS adds no new capability because the TS side already supersedes it.
notable_cells.S3.F4: still fails today because there is no actual shared session interchange caller in the live tree.
notable_cells.S4.F4: same demand gap; a bridge without a caller is speculative complexity.

S1_TS_to_hexa.loc_basis: 1461 TS LOC against 640 hexa LOC baseline
S1_TS_to_hexa.why: feature reduction is not mechanical; it is a redesign under tighter runtime constraints

S2_hexa_to_TS.why: but delivers near-zero value because TS already has the richer substrate

S3_shared_schema_only.why: best cost profile, but only if a real interchange need materializes

S4_bridge.why: integration cost is bounded but unjustified without a caller

axes.name: demand-signal.live-tree
findings: TS side is heavily load-bearing today: SessionManager.create/open/inMemory/list/fork flows are exercised across runtime and a broad test surface in `hive/packages/coding-agent`.
anima roadmap entry `TSRV-P1-1` asks only for a 30-turn session window with recovery, and that task is already marked done.

`clm_lore_serve.hexa` mirrors only the minimal in-memory session slice instead of importing `serving/session_manager.hexa`, which shows the anima side is still optimizing for narrow local use rather than richer shared session semantics.
No live hive<->anima cross-runtime consumer was found that needs compaction, branching, migration, labels, or JSONL tree replay.

roi_verdict.summary: The live 2026-04-27 tree still does not justify any direct session-manager port. The TS artifact is a rich tree-structured session engine that is deeply load-bearing in hive; the hexa artifact is a bounded rolling chat substrate designed around local runtime constraints. The cheapest valid future move remains S3: define a shared intersection schema only if a real cross-runtime consumer appears.

F1_direction_disambiguated.evidence: the cycle scored four concrete strategies rather than treating 'port' as a single ambiguous action

F2_feature_parity_not_overstated.evidence: direct-port strategies were rejected specifically because TS carries materially richer semantics than the hexa side consumes

F3_target_runtime_constraints_respected.evidence: hexa constraints in the file header were treated as structural design limits, not as minor implementation details

F4_caller_demand_evidenced.evidence: the absence of a cross-runtime caller was treated as a hard negative gate, preventing speculative promotion

F5_cheaper_alternative_not_dominant.evidence: S3 and simple no-port coexistence were named explicitly as cheaper alternatives that dominate port-positive motions under current demand

obstructions_documented.detail: `clm_lore_serve.hexa` embeds a minimal mirror of the session logic instead of importing `serving/session_manager.hexa`; the local anima dependency graph has not converged on the hexa session manager as a single reusable substrate.

Existing anima smoke artifacts record `serving/session_manager.hexa` compile failures around undeclared `split`, which is a maintenance signal against expanding the module's scope via an external port campaign.

next_round_handoff.task: Re-run this drill only when a real hive<->anima session interchange caller appears.
If that caller appears, start with S3: specify the intersection envelope `{role, content, timestamp, turn_id}` before any code port is proposed.
If anima first converges on `serving/session_manager.hexa` as a shared local substrate, refresh A4 demand-signal before reopening any port-positive thesis.

no_fabrication_evidence: read `session-manager.ts` lines 1-520 and grepped exported surface markers at lines 315, 482, 705, 1111, 1305-1352
read `session_manager.hexa` lines 1-520 and confirmed 30-turn rolling window, TSV persistence, and embedded self-tests
read `clm_lore_serve.hexa` lines 495-580 and confirmed a local mirrored minimal session section rather than import-based reuse
grepped `hive/packages/coding-agent/{src,test}` and confirmed broad live dependence on SessionManager runtime flows
grepped `anima` and `nexus/roadmaps` and found no cross-runtime consumer requiring TS session-manager semantics
honesty_triad.no_overreach: This witness does not claim that future schema-sharing would be bad; it claims that any current direct code port is unjustified.
honesty_triad.write_barrier: Only this witness JSON was modified in this cycle.
